title: armeria splendens subsp. bigerrensis
intro: Armeria splendens subsp. bigerrensis, commonly known as the Bigear Sea Thrift or Thrift, is a flowering plant species within the Plumbaginaceae family. It is a perennial plant with pink or white flowers and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The Bigear Sea Thrift can be prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in tinctures and salves, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
